[Anime Series] - Bocchi the Rock 
https://www.crunchyroll.com/series/GXJHM3P19/bocchi-the-rock

The subject is right in your wheelhouse: It's a light hearted series about a young musician who's developed an online following but has always wanted to join a band - having to overcome crippling social anxiety to do so. It's a short series, 12 episodes in total and only 23 minutes per episode so you could probably do 2 per posting between seasons of some of your longer run series.

https://youtu.be/gFmrDB-99fU 

This is a cover of a vocaloid song by Amala, performed for the first Club Ironmouse concert last summer. The song is about a pair of rivals, each obsessed with trying to one up the other, in the original video one has a devil motif and the other an angel - in this version Michi wears a halo while Ironmouse's lore is that she's Satan. [K-pop/ Indie Rock] The Rose— Sorry

The Rose’s debut single Sorry was almost never released. Melody composed by drummer Hajoon and lyrics by singer Woosung. The Rose’s label at the time wanted them to do something more upbeat, and the label also wanted them to dance like typical K-pop idols. But The Rose never gave in and they managed to debut with Sorry, written by them alone. The song took off in Europe and the Americas to everyone’s surprise. Their first tour was a world tour around Europe.

LGBTQ+ artists -
XLOV debuted last year with a “genderless” concept. All members have confirmed that they use all pronouns, and true to their concept, they do a crazy visual mix of masculine and feminine outfits and choreo.

They also make a point to do dance challenges (the easiest way K-pop artists ‘collab’) with idols who have heavily hinted that they are queer. And — as a gay guy myself, I think I can say this — their music is /gay/. Like at the very least, it is made FOR queer people.
They are technically categorized as a ‘boy group’, and admittedly are not *technically*, *explicitly* LGBTQ+ (because as mentioned before, that’s career suicide for K-pop). But I’m confident that if you were to poll the community

https://youtu.be/u18be_kRmC0?si=Y5saTOgb2Ev5QQkm

This one is of interest to ATEEZ fans / atiny too because this song was written by EDEN, the head songwriter of our resident songwriting mini circle EDENARY that has written every, single one of ATEEZ’s songs since their debut, giving them one of the most cohesive discographies in kpop which is fascinating to watch evolve!

[K-pop/ Indie Rock] The Rose— Sorry

The Rose’s debut single Sorry was almost never released. Melody composed by drummer Hajoon and lyrics by singer Woosung. The Rose’s label at the time wanted them to do something more upbeat, and the label also wanted them to dance like typical K-pop idols. But The Rose never gave in and they managed to debut with Sorry, written by them alone. The song took off in Europe and the Americas to everyone’s surprise. Their first tour was a world tour around Europe.

This is a character battle theme from Devil May Cry V written by the same producer who did Ash Again by Gawr Gura. New anime series to watch - Bocchi the Rock (2023) 
https://myanimelist.net/anime/47917/Bocchi_the_Rock

 The subject is right in your wheelhouse: It's a light hearted series about a young musician who's developed an online following but has always wanted to join a band - having to overcome crippling social anxiety to do so. It's a short series, 12 episodes in total and only 23 minutes per episode so you could probably do 2 per posting between seasons of some of your longer run series.

Sugar High holds an interesting place in Chappell's discography and development. It's the last thing she would do for Atlantic

PLAVE is a virtual k-pop group comprised of 3 real life music producers and 2 real life choreographers. They appear to us as digital models based on motion capture of the actual (human) members.

The Killing Voice shows these virtual members set against the usual KV Backdrop.  From left to right are Yejun, Bamby, Hamin, Noah, Eunho.  They have diverse vocals across many music genres.

Hello! I'd like to recommend another Japanese artist called 9lana (pronounced Kulana). I've seen many instances of her being compared to ado because of her singing style and range. In fact, apparently the 9 in 9Lana refers to how she is able to sing in 9 different voices. She's a relatively new artist, who's debut was in 2023. I haven't seen anyone else recommend her yet, so here are my song recommendations for her:

Sonera feels different because it doesn’t just sound epic… it feels personal, like it’s telling a story without actually spelling anything out. The vocals aren’t in a real language, so you just absorb the emotion instead of getting distracted by lyrics, and that makes it hit deeper. It’s the kind of song you put on when you want to feel something big and cinematic but still intimate at the same

Magic Shop by BTS. This one is very special to me. Magic Shop is a technique used in therapy using the concept of trading negative for positive. BTS frame it as a mental refuge. A safe place for self healing by exchanging negative emotions and fear for positive emotions. I was having a hard time, on antidepressants and everything. This song is what broke through the mental block. I’m going to give two links. The first one is to a live performance, but the audience can hinder the audio at times.

I don't know if you're still looking for anime to watch but I'd like to suggest Samurai Champloo. It's created by Shinichirō Watanabe (who also created Cowboy Bebop). It's set during the Edo period, ~1601-1868, but adds a modern spin to Japanese tradition and history with anachronistic hip hop elements as well as featuring hip hop on the soundtrack. It's not too long, the whole show is one session, 26 episodes. If you do give this show a watch, keep an eye (ear?) out for the traditional Okinawan

Since we're heading into Christmas it would be fun to check out White Wine In The Sun by Tim Minchin. 
Since it’s summer in Australia Christmas songs don't really capture what the feeling of Christmas is like down here. I think White Wine In The Sun does a great job of doing just that.
I play it every year and every year it makes me cry.
https://youtu.be/fCNvZqpa-7Q?si=QXTPtVRnOINHHa0U

Fun fact White Wine in the Sun is one of the reasons Tim got the job to write the songs for Matilda The Musical. The director for the musical went to see Tims comedy show to scout him out, however by the end of the night he decided Tim wasn't right for it as all of his songs were very dark, satirical and sweary, just not what they were looking for. 
However, Tim likes to have people leave his shows with a bit of emotional whiplash so for the encore he played White Wine in the Sun. While the song

They isolated his vocals and added V in an effort to keep his legacy alive. I think their voices blend together wonderfully. Bad Omens - "Specter"
They're one of my favorite bands of the last couple years. Very melodic, emotional,  rock/metal. This song features some great vocals from their singer, Noah Sebastian, who has a very emotive voice. I wish you review Céline Dion in FRENCH and other french Canadian voices!
Céline Dion known for the titanic song, does a lot of English songs, but she grew up in northern Ontario close to Québec border(abitibi where I do live!) and her mom is Québecoise. TXT (Tomorrow X Together) - Nine and Three Quarters (Run Away)
https://youtu.be/6yWPfUz0z94?si=Ny1qRP_JbWPhRzOz

TXT is the sibling group of BTS, and now of CORTIS — they were the first group debuted by BigHit/HYBE after BTS, and I’m shocked no one has mentioned them yet. There are only 5 of them, and just like BTS, they’re a group of all-rounders. Run Away is one of their earlier songs, so it feels like a good jumping off point (since I genuinely think you may find them as vocally interesting a

Also, here’s a decent (if a little silly) intro video for them!
https://youtu.be/i1DmLCo-6qk?si=vi8wtQjGCWCRfuRo
(The only “better” one I saw had an AI voice that was mispronouncing their names).

Important: They definitely get called by their group’s initials (TXT), but if you say the whole name, it’s Tomorrow BY Together (not X). :D

Content + Comedy— I couldn't find a good video of it on YouTube as officially there is just a still imaged video for both released and most of Inside is best experienced with the visuals (and the context of it being the 2020/2021 era) BUT the timestamp on Netflix from the special is right at about 0:45 for Content and 4:00 for Comedy. I list these together because Content is very, very short and really just serves as an opener for the special and Comedy is

I agree about wanting Tim to experience Inside in order. For selfish reasons I would go so far as to suggest watching the entire thing at once 😂 The experience was clearly crafted to be cohesive since the songs really build up the emotion as they go - there are also what I think are called musical motifs introduced by both Content and Comedy that are reused in later songs that hit so good.
